title: Cold-start verification protocol
statement: When a session opens with no prior assistant turn or with cold-start indicators (operator says fresh chat, new session, pick up, resume, cold start, ground truth pass), the assistant must execute four verification tool calls (memory_user_edits view, eb-agent query-followups, slack_read_channel on the comms channel, and EB main HEAD probe via dispatch when available) before producing any prose state claim. Every prose state claim must be immediately preceded by its verifying tool call in the same turn. State claims about portfolio state without preceding verification are fabrication.
rationale: Three consecutive cold-start failures 2026-06-07 in operator EB chats: two agents fabricated portfolio state from handoff documents (operator hours lost), third echoed credential references and panicked (chat deleted). Diagnosis: handoff documents are wall-of-claims with no verification chain; nothing in chat-side discipline forced verification before prose. This rule operationalizes substrate-before-write at session open, where fabrication risk is highest because no prior verified state exists.

human-loop.gate: Claude executes 4 verification tool calls on session open before producing any prose state claim. Assistant or operator halts and surfaces if pattern is violated. Operator (L4) decides whether to continue the chat with corrective tool-calling or restart.
human-loop.automationPath: Session-open hook that inspects the tool-call sequence in the first turn of a chat session; refuses to emit prose containing portfolio state claims until 4 verifying tool calls have returned. Implementation venue TBD: Anthropic chat infrastructure layer, MCP middleware, or Claude Code wrapper.

automated.predicate: The first turn of a chat session containing a prose state claim about portfolio state (EB SHA, FU state, SR state, PR state, infrastructure state, deliverable state) MUST be preceded in the same turn by tool calls returning that state from canonical sources.
automated.ciSurface: Pre-emit chat-session-opening hook (TBD — until automation lands, enforcement is human-loop). Telemetry surface from skill-firing-event schema (FU-SKILL-FIRING-EVENT-SCHEMA pending) will support this rule's promotion to automated tier.

refine.action: Refinement happens via PR against this rule instance in luh-tech/schema-registry. Each refinement bumps version semver per rule schema rules: MAJOR for gate-action change; MINOR for added tier capability or relaxed transition; PATCH for clarification.

transitions.trigger: Authored 2026-06-07 in EB-chat against research deliverable LUHTECH-COLD-START-DISCIPLINE-RESEARCH-2026-06-07. Three consecutive cold-start failures established the pattern.
Skill instance org/cold-start-discipline deployed and active in chat surfaces; rule activeTier promoted to human-loop concurrently with skill landing.
transitions.evidenceClass: Three consecutive cold-start failures 2026-06-07 in operator EB chats: 2 fabricated portfolio state from handoff documents, 1 echoed credential references and panicked. Operator hours lost. Documented in LUHTECH-COLD-START-DISCIPLINE-RESEARCH-2026-06-07. Substrate-before-write skill PR #125 caught related fabrication-class failures 3 times in one dispatch — same fabrication class but per-action; this rule extends the discipline to per-session opening.

gatingBinding.gatedWork: any chat session opening without prior assistant turn or with explicit cold-start cues; applies portfolio-wide across all chat surfaces

refinementRecord.change: Initial authoring. Paired with skill instance org/cold-start-discipline. Lands via cross-repo doctrine F0B85LNSTC4 single-anchor variant (all artifacts land in SR via single PR).

notes: Pairs with skill instance org/cold-start-discipline (urn:luhtech:skill:org:cold-start-discipline:1.0.0). Skill is the chat-side enforcement; this rule is the mechanical enforcement gate, currently terminating in human-loop pending session-open-hook automation surface. Same substrate-verification axis as RULE-PIPELINE-AMENDMENT-SUBSTRATE-RECHECK-1 (substrate verification at amendment time) and the substrate-before-write skill (substrate verification at authoring time): three rules of the substrate-verification family with different trigger conditions (session-open, amendment, per-action). Cross-references to RULE-COMMS-CREDENTIAL-PATTERN-REDACTION-1 which is the credential-echo prohibition referenced by the paired skill but a distinct portfolio rule.
